_comment: ADLC Salesforce pipeline allowlist — copied into each project's .claude/settings.json by /init. defaultMode is bypassPermissions because /sprint and /proceed run unattended pipelines that fire many routine commands; the allow list below is the safety contract. To require human prompts in your projects, set your user-level ~/.claude/settings.json defaultMode to 'acceptEdits' or 'plan' (project settings override user settings, so set it project-side here too if you want it everywhere). Pre-approves routine git/gh/sf CLI / sf-lint / lint-skills / Apex test / sf agent test / Vlocity preview operations. Also pre-approves common dev shell utilities (curl, brew, open, unzip, pip, perl, xmllint, pdftotext) and tool dispatchers (sf:* as a coarse fallback, vlocity packGetDiffs/packExport/packDeploy --simulate). Read scopes for ~/.claude/ (skills cache), ~/.adlc/ (runtime state), and /tmp + /private/tmp (synthetic fixtures, scratch worktrees) so devs do not get prompted for these paths every session. WebFetch is allowed for the canonical SFDC/Mule docs sites and GitHub raw — narrower than wildcard, broader than the per-project additions that historically piled up in settings.local.json. Production deploys (sf project deploy start, sf agent activate, sf agent publish), Vlocity packDeploy, and destructive data ops (sf data delete/update/upsert/import) currently live in allow for unattended pipeline runs — move them to ask if you want a human gate. git/gh/npm/sh use broad prefix wildcards (anything destructive there is already on the allow list explicitly); sf and vlocity stay granular so the safe/destructive split is auditable. Project-private paths (absolute /Users/<name>/... or ./.specify/* scripts) are intentionally NOT in the template — those belong in .claude/settings.local.json per project.
_hooks_comment: Stop / UserPromptSubmit hooks emit one JSONL event per turn boundary to ~/.adlc/runtime/user-wait.jsonl. The sprint dashboard tails this log to compute per-session/per-project/per-REQ user-wait time (the gap between Claude finishing a turn and you submitting the next prompt). No third-party deps; uses POSIX date and a single appendFile per event. Works inside git worktrees (CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR is set per-session by Claude Code).
